title: teline maderensis subsp. paivae
intro: Teline maderensis subsp. paivae, also known as the Madeiran teline or Paiva's teline, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of alkaloids and other compounds. The health benefits of Teline maderensis subsp. paivae include its potential to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The herbal preparations of Teline maderensis subsp. paivae typically involve infusions or decoctions of the leaves or roots, which can be used as teas or applied topically as a poultice or salve.
